name: 01_not_armed_preserves_count
description: Guard: when armed=False, the entire stop-check block is skipped. The inline producer NEITHER increments NOR resets below_stop_count when not armed. Count is preserved unchanged; hit is False.
derivation: armed=False -> the `if armed and not triggered:` guard fails on the FIRST clause. The producer's entire stop-check block (both the magnitude/MC condition path AND the reset-else branch) is skipped, so below_stop_count is neither incremented nor reset. Function returns (current_below_stop_count, False) = (2, False). The inputs would otherwise (if armed) trigger an increment: current_return -5.0 <= stop_trigger_level - 0.10 = -1.10 is TRUE, and prob_beating 40.0 < 60.0 is TRUE -- BUT armed=False blocks all of that.
